title: Distribution / Host Integration SSOT: Local IPC
summary: This SSOT fixes the C11 local IPC, helper-runtime, consent, and store-safe local execution boundaries as generated documentation.
The generated pages are bound to `internal/hostintegration`, local daemon IPC defaults, and store-distribution contract gates.

source_checks.name: local-ipc-default-endpoint
source_checks.contains: It does not open sockets or named pipes.

source_checks.name: helper-runtime-pure-boundary
source_checks.contains: It does not spawn providers, install

source_checks.name: external-provider-confirmation
source_checks.contains: auto-detected CLI should require confirmation in mac app store channel

source_checks.name: msix-store-named-pipe
source_checks.contains: msix-store background should be allowed with consent and review approval

source_checks.name: msix-sideload-broker
source_checks.contains: msix-sideload background should only require explicit consent

source_checks.name: consent-ledger-model
source_checks.contains: records; the current state is always derived.

source_checks.name: consent-subject-validation
source_checks.contains: provider execute consent must not include workspace id

source_checks.name: workspace-grant-consent
source_checks.contains: grant without workspace-access consent should not materialize

assertions: C11 local IPC exposes endpoint descriptors, not listener implementation details.
Helper runtime plans compute store-safe roles, startup registration, background rules, and review surfaces without spawning providers or calling OS APIs.
Provider CLIs remain external user-installed executables; store-safe routing is represented as C11 records and policy input.
macOS Store helper execution requires explicit consent and Store review approval before background execution is allowed.
Windows MSIX helper execution uses named-pipe local IPC and keeps Store versus sideload update/review rules distinct.
Consent is an append-only ledger whose current state is derived and then consumed by helper/runtime/workspace materialization decisions.
